name: sidecar-lint
description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in repos with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking — emits additionalContext for: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15), hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths (commons @D g13 · sidecar @D s3), marketplace.json plugin entry ↔ each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22), hook .sh missing user-exec bit, missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29), and — when the repo carries .claude-plugin/profiles.json (sidecar's multi-user enable profiles) — any marketplace plugin missing a tier there (sidecar @D s7: an untagged plugin silently defaults to 'personal', hidden from the minimal/hexa profiles), and any registered STDIO MCP-server plugin (project.tape @D s15 — `mcps/` source or command-based `mcpServers`; STDIO MCP forbidden because its stdin/stdout JSON-RPC handshake collides with hexa hook stdio + fake-stdin; an HTTP/SSE-transport `mcpServers` carrying a `url` is PERMITTED and not flagged). 0.9.0 — s15 relaxed to allow HTTP/SSE MCP; the MCP check now flags only stdio (url/http/sse marker present → allowed). 0.8.1 — the top-level-dir check (project.tape @D s16: co-locate by owner/domain) is now an ALLOWLIST, not a name-blocklist: any TRACKED top-level dir that is NOT a concept dir (`hooks`·`commands`·`skills`·`agents`) / `bin` / `.github` / `.claude-plugin` is flagged, so renaming a bucket (`docs`→`documentation`, `state`→`tmp`) does NOT dodge it. The advisory directs co-location under the owning domain/plugin. Tracked-only (`git ls-files`), so gitignored runtime/scratch (`state/`·`drafts/`·`build/`) never false-fires. Self-excludes its own files + CHANGELOG.md. NO opt-out by design — no env var, no config file, no exception list.
